heading: Information, witnesses, and evidence
label: 42104 Information, witnesses, and evidence

children.heading: .—Order To Supply Information
children.label: a .—Order To Supply Information
children.text: In carrying out , the Federal Maritime Commission may order any person (including a common carrier, tramp operator, bulk operator, shipper, shippers’ association, ocean transportation intermediary, or marine terminal operator, or an officer, receiver, trustee, lessee, agent, or employee thereof) to file with the Commission a report, answers to questions, documentary material, or other information the Commission considers necessary or appropriate. The Commission may require the response to any such order to be made under oath. The response shall be provided in the form and within the time specified by the Commission.section 42101 of this title
authorize a party to use depositions, written interrogatories, and discovery procedures that, to the extent practicable, conform to the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure (28 App. U.S.C.).

children.heading: .—Witness Fees
children.label: c .—Witness Fees
children.text: Unless otherwise prohibited by law, and subject to funds being appropriated, a witness in a proceeding under is entitled to the same fees and mileage as in the courts of the United States.section 42101 of this title

after notice and opportunity for a hearing, suspend tariffs and service contracts of a common carrier or the common carrier’s right to use tariffs of conferences and service contracts of agreements of which it is a member; or
assess a civil penalty of not more than $5,000 for each day that the information is not provided.

children.heading: .—Enforcement
children.label: e .—Enforcement
children.text: If a person does not comply with an order or subpoena of the Commission under this section, the Commission may seek enforcement in a district court of the United States having jurisdiction over the parties. If, after hearing, the court determines that the order or subpoena was regularly made and duly issued, the court shall enforce the order or subpoena.
